Title: The Arabidopsis UDP-glycosyltransferases UGT79B2 and UGT79B3, contribute to cold, salt and drought stress tolerance via modulating anthocyanin accumulation

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anthocyanins function as antioxidants and contribute to abiotic stress tolerance
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: tt18 mutant background was used to show that UGT79B2/B3-dependent stress tolerance requires anthocyanin biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic background test using UGT79B2/B3 overexpression in tt18 mutant
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of ANS blocks anthocyanin biosynthesis and abolishes stress tolerance gain from UGT79B2/B3 overexpression
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. tt18 lacks anthocyanin biosynthesis because ANS is disrupted. 2. UGT79B2/B3 overexpression in tt18 fails to improve cold, salt and osmotic stress tolerance. 3. This genetic evidence shows UGT79B2/B3-mediated stress adaptation depends on anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: tt18 is stress sensitive under tested conditions

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Quercetin/flavonol branch metabolites are not required for UGT79B2/B3-conferred stress tolerance in this study
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a branch-specific mutant to test whether quercetin biosynthesis is needed for UGT79B2/B3 stress phenotype; UGT79B2/B3 overexpression still improves tolerance in tt7
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic background test using UGT79B2/B3 overexpression in tt7 mutant
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Disruption of quercetin biosynthesis does not abolish stress tolerance conferred by UGT79B2/B3 overexpression
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. tt7 blocks quercetin metabolism and is stress sensitive. 2. Overexpression of UGT79B2/B3 in tt7 still markedly improves adaptation to cold, salt and osmotic stress. 3. Therefore quercetin biosynthesis is not required for the UGT79B2/B3-mediated stress-protective effect.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: tt7 is stress sensitive under tested conditions

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S (constitutive); native UGT79B2 promoter and mutated promoter::GUS constructs
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray data queried via Genevestigator; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Shows strongest activity toward cyanidin, about twofold higher than toward cyanidin 3-O-glucoside; only very low activity toward quercetin and kaempferol; no evident effect on flavonol/lignin-related metabolites in planta; stress tolerance effect is lost in tt18 background, indicating dependence on anthocyanin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers rhamnose from UDP-rhamnose to cyanidin and cyanidin 3-O-glucoside to form corresponding rhamnosides
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 35S overexpression; RNAi and CRISPR-Cas9 double-loss lines; recombinant protein enzyme assay; substrate screening; LC-MS product identification; endogenous metabolite profiling by HPLC/LC-MS; tt18 genetic background test
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant; scavenges ROS; promotes tolerance to cold, salt and drought/osmotic stress
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: anthocyanin modification branch of flavonoid/phenylpropanoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for anthocyanin accumulation under stress, not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms modified anthocyanin products and promotes target anthocyanin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT79B2 is strongly induced by cold, salt, drought/osmotic and ABA treatments. 2. UGT79B2 overexpression increases freezing, salt and drought tolerance, while double loss of UGT79B2/UGT79B3 causes stress sensitivity. 3. Recombinant UGT79B2 uses UDP-rhamnose to rhamnosylate cyanidin and cyanidin 3-O-glucoside. 4. Overexpression increases anthocyanin accumulation and antioxidant capacity, whereas mutants reduce both. 5. Stress tolerance conferred by UGT79B2 depends on an intact anthocyanin pathway, as tt18 abolishes the benefit.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced in 2-week-old seedlings by cold (strongest), NaCl, ABA and mannitol; stress-induced expression under abiotic conditions
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious penalty reported; no major changes detected in flavonol or lignin-related metabolites measured
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Anthocyanin biosynthesis / flavonoid biosynthesis / phenylpropanoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold (4°C/freezing), NaCl, mannitol, drought, ABA, constant light, high sucrose
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by abiotic stresses, especially cold
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering stress-tolerant plants by enhancing anthocyanin accumulation; should be deployed where anthocyanin biosynthetic capacity is intact and may be combined with upstream anthocyanin pathway genes/regulators
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation and enhances tolerance to cold, salt and drought/osmotic stress

Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray data queried via Genevestigator; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Biochemically similar to UGT79B2; very low activity toward quercetin and kaempferol; stress tolerance effect abolished in tt18 but retained in tt7, supporting anthocyanin-centered function
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers rhamnose from UDP-rhamnose to cyanidin and cyanidin 3-O-glucoside to form corresponding rhamnosides
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 35S overexpression; RNAi and CRISPR-Cas9 double-loss lines; recombinant protein enzyme assay; LC-MS product identification; endogenous anthocyanin profiling; tt18 and tt7 background tests
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant; scavenges ROS; promotes tolerance to cold, salt and drought/osmotic stress
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: anthocyanin modification branch of flavonoid/phenylpropanoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for stress-associated anthocyanin accumulation, not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms modified anthocyanin products and promotes target anthocyanin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT79B3 is strongly induced by cold and other abiotic stresses. 2. UGT79B3 overexpression improves freezing, salt and drought tolerance, while double loss with UGT79B2 increases sensitivity. 3. Recombinant UGT79B3 specifically uses UDP-rhamnose to rhamnosylate cyanidin and cyanidin 3-O-glucoside. 4. Overexpression elevates anthocyanin accumulation and antioxidant capacity. 5. The stress-protective role requires anthocyanin biosynthesis and is not dependent on quercetin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced in seedlings by cold (strongest), NaCl, ABA and mannitol
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious penalty reported; no major changes detected in measured flavonol/lignin-related compounds
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Anthocyanin biosynthesis / flavonoid biosynthesis / phenylpropanoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold (4°C/freezing), NaCl, mannitol, drought, ABA, constant light, high sucrose
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by abiotic stresses, especially cold
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising metabolic engineering target to improve abiotic stress tolerance through anthocyanin enhancement; best used in backgrounds with functional anthocyanin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation and enhances tolerance to cold, salt and drought/osmotic stress

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: anthocyanin biosynthesis/modification during cold stress response
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant; ROS scavenging; contributes to cold stress tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Cold-induced transcription factor involved in cold acclimation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Directly binds DRE element near -42 bp in UGT79B2 promoter and enriches UGT79B2/UGT79B3 promoter fragments in ChIP; CBF3 does not substitute for this regulation in this study
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Paper proposes feedback regulation linking anthocyanin consumption/accumulation and CBF1-directed pathway activation, but this is presented as a model rather than directly proven
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential upstream engineering target to enhance cold-induced anthocyanin accumulation and stress tolerance, though broader pleiotropic cold-response effects should be considered
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates UGT79B2 and UGT79B3; associated with increased expression of anthocyanin biosynthetic genes CHS, CHI, F3H, DFR and ANS under cold
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Cold-responsive; overexpression elevates downstream target expression under cold
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation under cold and enhances expression of UGT79B2/B3 and anthocyanin pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter mutagenesis-GUS assay; EMSA; yeast one-hybrid; ChIP-qPCR; overexpression and knockout expression analysis; anthocyanin measurements in overexpression and mutant plants
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CBF1 directly binds a proximal DRE element in the UGT79B2 promoter and associates with UGT79B2/UGT79B3 promoters in planta. 2. CBF1 overexpression enhances, and cbf1 mutation suppresses, UGT79B2/B3 induction by cold. 3. Altering CBF1 expression changes anthocyanin accumulation and expression of anthocyanin biosynthetic genes under cold. 4. CBF1 links cold signaling to anthocyanin metabolism through UGT79B2/B3.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: cold-responsive direct activator linking upstream cold signaling to anthocyanin accumulation via UGT79B2/B3
